calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-13-2006 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Nancy N. Lawlor, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff. The court having reviewed the file finds no good cause or substancial justification for Cross-Complainant's failure to comply with the court's orders and rules. Therefore, It is hereby ordered that Cross-Complaint filed by Cross-Complainant in Propria Persona be dismissed. The court finds that a less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance. Court ordered Case dismissed in its entirety at the request of Plaintiff's counsel Proceedings reported by: Helen Sorep-Matley CSR 225. (212)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-04-2006 Continued To Mar-13-06 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-07-2005 Continued To Jan-09-06 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212
MINI MINUTES: Appearance for Plaintiff. Defendant and Cross-Complainant in Propria Persona is sanctioned $350.00, for failure to appear. The OSC is re-set to 1/9/2006 at 1;30 PM, in Department 212. Proceedings reported by: Leticia Perez. (212) Written sanction order not issued/Response to OSC filed on 11/3/05 not available. (212)

MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
Off Calendar Oct-04-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew Dimitriou for plaintiff and moving party Kevin D. Mitchell

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories set #1 and #2, special interrogatories set #1, and request for production set #1 is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objection within 21 days of notice of this order. Failure to respond was misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $836.30 are to be paid within 21 days of notice of this order. ORDER SIGNED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-02-2005 Continued To Nov-07-05 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Neither the discovery that is at issue nor the exhibits referenced in the declaration of counsel are attached to the motion. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
